case_title: MARCELO MONTEIRO VS. GOLDLINE INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al

docket_entries.description: 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MONTEIRO, MARCELO AS TO DEFENDANT GOLDLINE INTERNATIONAL, INC. DOES 1 TO 50

NOTICE AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF RECEIPT TO DEFT: GOLDLINE INTERNATIONAL, INC. A COPY OF THE SUMMONS & COMPLT; NTC OF CMS; "BLANK" CMS; ADR PRGM INFO PKG; AND "BLANK" SITP TO ADR FILED BY PLAINTIFF MONTEIRO, MARCELO

LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GOLDLINE INTERNATIONAL, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 4 BECAUSE IT IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS; SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 THROUGH 3 IF PLAINTIFF CAN PLEAD IN GOOD FAITH A VIOLATION OF THE TERMS OF THE WRITTEN CONTRACT AND A VIABLE BASIS FOR RELIEF UNDER SECTION 17200. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB
ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT GOLDLINE INTERNATIONAL, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ADEQUETALY PLEADED FRAUDULENT CONDUCT FOR PURPOSES OF SECTION 17200 GIVEN THE ALLEGATIONS OF POST-CONTRACT REPRESENTATIONS, 10 DAYS TO RESPOND. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Stephanie Blazewicz, Esq. of Bryan & cave, 415-675-3418 for defendant and moving party Goldine International, Inc.
Court Call Appearance: Adam Ramirez, Esq. of Hakeem, Ellis & Marengo, 209-474-2800 for plaintiff and opposing party Marcello Monteiro

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT GOLDLINE INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ADEQUETALY PLEADED FRAUDULENT CONDUCT FOR PURPOSES OF SECTION 17200 GIVEN THE ALLEGATIONS OF POST-CONTRACT REPRESENTATIONS. 10 DAYS TO RESPOND. Prevailing party to submit form of order.
